questions.question: Define Public Cloud and Private Cloud. Explain two key differences between them in terms of ownership, security, and scalability.
keyPoints: Public Cloud: Services offered over the internet by third-party providers
Private Cloud: Dedicated infrastructure for a single organization
Security: Shared resources with isolation vs dedicated secure environment

questions.question: What is a Community Cloud? Describe its characteristics and provide two suitable use cases where community clouds are preferably deployed.
keyPoints: Shared infrastructure among organizations with common requirements
Use cases: Healthcare consortia, government agencies, research institutions
Addresses specific industry compliance and regulatory needs

questions.question: Explain the Hybrid Cloud deployment model. Discuss its major advantages, key challenges in implementation, and identify three business scenarios where organizations would benefit most from adopting a hybrid cloud strategy.
keyPoints: Combines public and private cloud infrastructure with data/app portability
Advantages: Scalability, cost optimization, business continuity, flexibility
Challenges: Data integration, network latency, security compliance, management complexity
Scenarios: Bursting workloads, disaster recovery, sensitive data with public services

questions.question: Create a comprehensive comparison table between Public, Private, Hybrid, and Community clouds based on: ownership, management responsibility, scalability, security level, and cost structure. Based on this analysis, recommend the most appropriate cloud deployment model for: (a) A multi-specialty hospital handling patient data, (b) A tech startup with limited budget, (c) A state government department, (d) A multinational corporation with global operations.
keyPoints: Public: Third-party owned, low cost, high scalability, moderate security
Private: Organization-owned, high control, limited scalability, highest security
Hybrid: Combined model, balanced scalability, variable security, moderate cost
Community: Shared among specific groups, collaborative management, compliance-oriented
